description: Host can create a game and receive a 6-character join code
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/game returns a game ID and a unique 6-character alphanumeric join code
Join code is unique across all active games
Game appears in server-side active games map
Response includes WebSocket connection URL for the host

description: Players can join a game using the join code and choose a display name
acceptance_criteria: Player enters join code and display name, receives confirmation and WebSocket connection
Host sees new player appear in real time via WebSocket
Duplicate display names within the same game are rejected with an error message
Invalid join codes return an appropriate error
Players cannot join a game that has already started

description: Host can configure and start the game from the lobby
acceptance_criteria: Host sees all connected players listed in the lobby UI
Host can start the game only when player count is between 5 and 15
Starting the game transitions all connected clients to the game phase
Players who disconnect from the lobby are removed from the player list
human_review: Verify lobby UI layout is clear and intuitive

description: Server assigns roles according to the official Trouble Brewing distribution table
acceptance_criteria: Role distribution matches the official table for every player count (5-15)
Each player receives exactly one role
All 14 Trouble Brewing roles are available in the role pool
Townsfolk, Outsider, Minion, and Demon counts match the table for the given player count
Random assignment does not repeat roles within a single game

description: Players receive their private role card after assignment
acceptance_criteria: Each player's client receives only their own role via WebSocket (not other players' roles)
Role card displays role name, team, and ability description
Storyteller's Grimoire shows all players' true roles
human_review: Verify role card UI is readable and correctly styled

description: Night 0 setup: Baron adjustment adds 2 Outsiders
acceptance_criteria: When Baron is in the game, 2 Townsfolk slots are replaced by 2 Outsider slots
The adjusted distribution still totals the correct player count
Baron adjustment is applied before role assignment, not after

description: Night 0 setup: Drunk is assigned an apparent Townsfolk role
acceptance_criteria: Drunk's apparent role is a Townsfolk role not otherwise in the game
Drunk's client shows the apparent role (not 'Drunk')
Storyteller's Grimoire shows both the true role (Drunk) and the apparent role
Drunk's apparent role is stored in game state for use by the ability system

description: Night 0 setup: Fortune Teller red herring is assigned
acceptance_criteria: One Good player is designated as the Fortune Teller's red herring at game start
Red herring assignment is stored in game state and visible in the Grimoire
Red herring does not change for the remainder of the game, even if the player dies

description: Night 0 setup: Minion and Demon info exchange
acceptance_criteria: Minions learn each other's identity and the Demon's identity on Night 1
Demon learns the Minions' identities on Night 1
Demon learns 3 not-in-play Townsfolk roles (bluff roles) on Night 1
Info is delivered via WebSocket to the correct players only

description: Dawn announcement: deaths from the previous night are announced
acceptance_criteria: Players who died during the night are announced by name (not role) at dawn
All connected clients receive the death announcement simultaneously
Dead players are marked as dead in the UI and cannot take day actions (except ghost vote)
If no deaths occurred, a 'no deaths' message is shown

description: Discussion phase with Storyteller-controlled timing
acceptance_criteria: Discussion phase begins after dawn announcement
Storyteller can end the discussion phase manually at any time
Players see a visual indicator that discussion is ongoing
Transitioning out of discussion moves to the nomination window

description: Nomination system: living players can nominate other players
acceptance_criteria: Any living player may nominate any other living player
A player may only nominate once per day
A player may only be nominated once per day
Storyteller can open and close the nomination window
Dead players cannot nominate

description: Voting system: simultaneous public voting on nominations
acceptance_criteria: Each nomination triggers a vote immediately
Living players can vote by clicking a vote button
Votes are locked in simultaneously (all players submit before results are revealed)
Vote results are revealed to all players at once
A nomination passes if the vote count exceeds half of living players (rounded up)
Dead players have exactly one ghost vote for the entire game; once used, they cannot vote again
Ghost vote usage is tracked and enforced server-side

description: Execution resolution: highest passing vote count triggers execution
acceptance_criteria: The player with the highest passing vote count is executed at end of day
If two nominations tie with the same passing count, no execution occurs
Executed player is marked as dead
Execution triggers win condition checks (Saint, Demon death)
If no nominations pass, no execution occurs

description: Storyteller can end the day and transition to night
acceptance_criteria: Storyteller has an 'End Day' button that transitions to night phase
End Day is only available after the nomination window is closed
All clients transition to night phase view simultaneously

description: Ability execution context with isPoisoned and isDrunk flags
acceptance_criteria: AbilityContext type includes isPoisoned and isDrunk boolean fields
All ability resolution functions receive AbilityContext as a parameter
A poisoned ability returns false/null/corrupted information instead of true information
A drunk ability behaves identically to a poisoned ability (corrupted output)
Context flags are set by the state machine based on active Poisoner target and Drunk status
The corruption flag is on the ability execution context, not on the role itself

description: Night order is defined as static data and queue is generated at runtime
acceptance_criteria: Night order is defined in src/data/nightOrder.ts as static data arrays
Night 1 order and subsequent night order are separate arrays
Night queue is generated at runtime, filtered to roles present and alive in the current game
Night order matches the official Trouble Brewing order

description: Storyteller night dashboard: sequential prompts for each role in the queue
acceptance_criteria: Storyteller sees each role's prompt in sequence during the night
Queue does not advance until the Storyteller confirms each step
Storyteller dashboard shows relevant information for each role's ability
Storyteller can provide input (selections, overrides) for each role prompt
human_review: Verify night dashboard UI is clear and shows the right info per role

description: End Night: Storyteller confirms, changes are committed, dawn fires
acceptance_criteria: Storyteller has an 'End Night' button that commits all night changes
All Storyteller actions within a night phase are reversible until End Night is confirmed
After End Night, night changes are committed to the game log and become permanent
Dawn announcement fires after End Night is confirmed

description: Storyteller override: can override any game event before it is committed
acceptance_criteria: Storyteller can override any night action result before confirming End Night
Override hooks exist at every phase transition and ability resolution step
Storyteller's action always takes precedence over automated logic
Override history is recorded in the game log

description: Grimoire: Storyteller's private dashboard with full game state
acceptance_criteria: Grimoire shows every player's true role and alive/dead status
Grimoire shows the Drunk's apparent role
Grimoire shows every player's current poison status
Grimoire shows the Fortune Teller's red herring player
Grimoire shows Scarlet Woman trigger status
Grimoire shows ghost vote tracking (which dead players have used their vote)
Grimoire shows the current night queue and position
Grimoire has a free-text notes field for the Storyteller
Grimoire is never visible to players (server enforces this)
human_review: Verify Grimoire layout is usable during fast-paced gameplay

description: All win conditions fire correctly and in priority order
acceptance_criteria: Saint executed: Good loses immediately at the moment of execution (if Saint is not poisoned)
Demon executed: Good wins at the moment of execution
Scarlet Woman trigger: if Demon dies and SW ability triggers (5+ alive, not poisoned), SW becomes Imp and game continues
Mayor win: if 3 players alive, no execution occurred, Mayor alive and not poisoned, Good wins
Evil win: if only 2 players remain alive at dawn after night kills, Evil wins
Win condition checks run in priority order (Saint > Demon > SW > Mayor > Evil)
Game transitions to end state, reveals all roles, announces winning team

description: Washerwoman: Night 1 only, learns one of two players is a specific Townsfolk
acceptance_criteria: Fires on Night 1 only via AbilityContext
Storyteller is prompted to choose two players and a Townsfolk role to reveal
One of the two players must actually be that Townsfolk role
Player receives the two-player-and-role information via WebSocket
If poisoned or drunk (context.isPoisoned || context.isDrunk), Storyteller provides false information

description: Librarian: Night 1 only, learns one of two players is a specific Outsider
acceptance_criteria: Fires on Night 1 only via AbilityContext
Storyteller is prompted to choose two players and an Outsider role to reveal
One of the two must actually be that Outsider role
If no Outsiders in the game, Librarian learns 'no Outsiders'
If poisoned or drunk, Storyteller provides false information

description: Investigator: Night 1 only, learns one of two players is a specific Minion
acceptance_criteria: Fires on Night 1 only via AbilityContext
Storyteller is prompted to choose two players and a Minion role to reveal
One of the two must actually be that Minion role
Recluse may be shown as the Minion (Storyteller's choice)
If poisoned or drunk, Storyteller provides false information

description: Chef: Night 1 only, learns number of evil pairs sitting adjacent
acceptance_criteria: Fires on Night 1 only via AbilityContext
Returns the number of pairs of Evil players sitting adjacent to each other around the circle
Seating order is determined by join order or Storyteller arrangement
If poisoned or drunk, returns a false number

description: Empath: each night, learns how many living neighbors are Evil
acceptance_criteria: Fires every night via AbilityContext
Returns 0, 1, or 2 based on how many of the Empath's two living neighbors are Evil
If a neighbor dies, the next living player in that direction becomes the neighbor
Neighbors are computed dynamically each night based on current alive state
Recluse may register as Evil at Storyteller's discretion
If poisoned or drunk, returns a false number

description: Fortune Teller: each night, chooses two players, learns if either is the Demon
acceptance_criteria: Fires every night via AbilityContext
Player chooses any two players
Returns yes if either chosen player is the Demon OR is the designated red herring
Red herring always returns yes even if dead
Recluse may register as Demon at Storyteller's discretion
If poisoned or drunk, may return false information

description: Undertaker: each night after first, learns the role of the player executed that day
acceptance_criteria: Fires every night except Night 1 via AbilityContext
If a player was executed the previous day, Undertaker learns their true role
If no execution occurred, Undertaker learns nothing
If poisoned or drunk, may receive false role information

description: Monk: each night (not first), chooses a player to protect from the Demon
acceptance_criteria: Fires every night except Night 1 via AbilityContext
Player chooses any player other than themselves
Protected player cannot be killed by the Demon that night
Monk cannot protect themselves
If poisoned, protection has no effect (target can be killed)

description: Ravenkeeper: if killed by the Demon at night, chooses a player and learns their role
acceptance_criteria: Triggers only if the Ravenkeeper is killed by the Demon at night
Upon triggering, Ravenkeeper immediately chooses any player
Ravenkeeper learns the chosen player's true role
If poisoned when killed, may receive false role information
Does not trigger if Ravenkeeper dies by execution

description: Virgin: first time nominated by a Townsfolk, that Townsfolk is executed
acceptance_criteria: One-time passive ability that triggers on the first nomination of the Virgin
If the nominator is a Townsfolk (including the Drunk's apparent role), the nominator is immediately executed
After triggering (successfully or not), the ability is spent
If poisoned when nominated, the ability does not trigger
The Drunk's nomination counts as Townsfolk (uses apparent role)

description: Slayer: once per game during the day, can attempt to slay the Demon
acceptance_criteria: One-time active ability usable during the day phase
Slayer publicly declares target
If target is the Demon, target dies immediately
If target is not the Demon, nothing happens
Ability is spent regardless of outcome
If poisoned, the ability has no effect even if targeting the Demon

description: Soldier: cannot be killed by the Demon at night
acceptance_criteria: Passive ability: Demon's night kill has no effect on the Soldier
If the Soldier is poisoned, they lose this protection and can be killed
Protection only applies to the Demon's kill, not execution or other effects

description: Mayor: bounced kill and 3-player no-execution Good win
acceptance_criteria: If only 3 players alive and no execution occurs that day, Good wins (if Mayor alive and not poisoned)
If Mayor is killed by Demon at night, Storyteller may redirect the kill to another player
Redirect target is chosen by the Storyteller before dawn
If Mayor is poisoned, neither ability applies

description: Butler: each night chooses a master; can only vote when master votes
acceptance_criteria: Each night, Butler chooses a player (not themselves) as their master
The following day, Butler may only vote if their master is also voting on the same nomination
Butler's vote token is locked until the master votes or the nomination closes
If the master dies before a vote, the Butler votes freely (constraint lifted)
Master alive status is checked at vote time, not at start of day
If poisoned or drunk, the Butler may vote freely

description: Drunk: believes they are a Townsfolk, but their ability always produces corrupted output
acceptance_criteria: Drunk's client shows their apparent Townsfolk role (never 'Drunk')
Drunk's apparent ability fires in the night queue as normal
All results produced by the Drunk's ability are corrupted (context.isDrunk is true)
Storyteller manually provides false information for the Drunk's night result
Drunk's night prompt flags to the Storyteller that this player is the Drunk
Drunk's apparent role is a Townsfolk not otherwise in the game

description: Recluse: may register as Evil to detection abilities at Storyteller's discretion
acceptance_criteria: Recluse may register as Evil or as a Minion role to detection abilities
Storyteller decides case-by-case whether the Recluse registers as Evil
Affected abilities: Fortune Teller, Investigator, Empath, Chef
Recluse is actually Good and on the Good team for win conditions
System presents the Storyteller with an option to mark the Recluse as Evil for each relevant check

description: Saint: if executed, Good loses immediately
acceptance_criteria: If the Saint is executed by the town, Good loses immediately
Loss triggers at the moment of execution, before any other end-of-day checks
If the Saint is poisoned, execution proceeds normally without triggering the loss
Saint dying at night (Demon kill) does not trigger the loss

description: Poisoner: each night, poisons a player (corrupts their ability)
acceptance_criteria: Fires every night (including Night 1) via AbilityContext
Poisoner chooses any player to poison
Poison applies from the moment the Poisoner acts in the night queue
Poison lasts until the Poisoner acts again the following night
A poisoned player's ability produces false/ineffective results
Poison status is visible in the Grimoire
Poisoner's identity is known to the Demon on Night 1

description: Spy: may see the Grimoire and register as Good to detection abilities
acceptance_criteria: Spy may see the full Grimoire (all role assignments, alive/dead, poison, drunk status)
Grimoire view is shown by the Storyteller during the Spy's night prompt
Spy may register as Good or as a Townsfolk/Outsider to detection abilities (Storyteller's choice)
Spy is on the Evil team for win conditions
Spy's identity is known to the Demon on Night 1

description: Scarlet Woman: becomes the Demon if the Demon dies with 5+ players alive
acceptance_criteria: If the Demon dies and there are 5 or more players alive at the moment of death, Scarlet Woman becomes the new Imp
Alive count is checked BEFORE applying the Demon's death
Transformation is secret; only the Storyteller knows
Good does not win when Scarlet Woman trigger activates
If the Scarlet Woman is poisoned when the Demon dies, the ability does not trigger
Scarlet Woman does NOT trigger on Imp star-pass (only on actual Demon death)

description: Baron: setup ability adds 2 Outsiders to the game (replaces 2 Townsfolk)
acceptance_criteria: Baron's ability fires once at game start during role assignment
2 Townsfolk slots are replaced by 2 Outsider slots
Baron has no night ability
Adjusted distribution still totals the correct player count

description: Imp: kills one player per night, can star-pass to a Minion
acceptance_criteria: Fires every night except Night 1 via AbilityContext
Imp chooses a player to kill; target is marked dead at dawn
If Imp targets a Monk-protected player, the kill has no effect
If Imp targets the Soldier (not poisoned), the kill has no effect
If Imp targets the Mayor, Storyteller may redirect the kill
If Imp kills themselves (star-pass), they die and a Minion of Storyteller's choice becomes the new Imp
Star-pass updates role assignments in the Grimoire
Scarlet Woman does NOT trigger on star-pass
If Imp is poisoned, the kill does not resolve

description: Poisoner timing: poison lasts from Poisoner's night action until next night
acceptance_criteria: A player poisoned on Night N is poisoned for all of Night N (including abilities after Poisoner in queue) and all of Day N
Poison expires when Night N+1 begins (unless re-poisoned)
Poison status is correctly computed for abilities firing after the Poisoner in the queue

description: Drunk + detection abilities: Storyteller manually provides false info
acceptance_criteria: When the Drunk's apparent ability fires, the Storyteller is flagged that this player is the Drunk
Storyteller manually enters false information for the Drunk's ability result
The Drunk receives the Storyteller-provided false info, not auto-computed info

description: Virgin + Drunk nominator: Drunk counts as Townsfolk for Virgin trigger
acceptance_criteria: If the Drunk nominates the Virgin, the Virgin's ability triggers (Drunk's apparent role is Townsfolk)
The Drunk is executed as a result
The system uses the apparent role (not true role) for the Virgin check

description: Single authoritative state machine: all mutations go through gameStateMachine.ts
acceptance_criteria: All game state mutations pass through a single server-side state machine
Client is read-only; receives state diffs over WebSocket and renders them
No client-side code mutates game state directly
State machine is the single source of truth for all game data
human_review: Audit codebase to confirm no client-side state mutations exist

description: Role file structure: each role is a self-contained module
acceptance_criteria: Each role is implemented in src/roles/[roleName].ts
Each module exports: role metadata (name, team, type), night order position, ability handler
No role file imports from another role file
Cross-role interactions are handled by the state machine
human_review: Audit role files to confirm no cross-role imports

description: Player client: role card view, day/night phase indicators, vote UI
acceptance_criteria: Player sees their role card with name, team, and ability description
Day/night phase transitions are visually indicated
Vote UI shows nomination, allows voting, and reveals results
Dead players are visually distinguished from alive players
Player cannot see other players' roles
human_review: Verify overall player UI is usable and clear

description: Seating circle: players arranged in a circle reflecting game seating
acceptance_criteria: Players are displayed in a circle layout on all clients
Seating order is consistent across all clients
Dead players are visually marked in the circle
Circle layout is used for adjacency calculations (Chef, Empath)
human_review: Verify circle layout renders correctly at different player counts

description: Full 7-player Trouble Brewing game completes start to finish
acceptance_criteria: 7 players can create a game, join, receive roles, and play through night and day phases
Night abilities fire in order and produce correct output
Day voting and execution work correctly
Win conditions trigger correctly
Game ends with role reveal and winner announcement
No console errors during the full game
human_review: Play through a full game manually to verify overall experience
